rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Not an article surface; this is a module-level file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Training creator's name banned in student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 66: 'Antti' appears as capstone-close signature. Documented in maintainer block (lines 70–72) as deliberate exception per rule 6's second carve-out (added 2026-05-03) for capstone-module closes. M6 is the AE101 core arc's final module; the close earns the human voice that practitioner-fluency mood warrants. Exception is in-scope-by-design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Three-pattern' appears throughout body (lines 4, 14, 23, 40); term was earned and named in M5's closing lecture (upstream module). This is continuation of an upstream-earned teaching term, not a maintainer-vocabulary leak. Maintainer block (line 141) confirms attribution: 'Ronacher's three-pattern — Armin Ronacher. Re-named in the closing lecture as a scaffold the student is now ready to sharpen, not a fixed recipe.'
Not a reference artefact; this is a module-level file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two 'Remember:' statements appear in Key Concepts section (lines 40, 44). These are structural concept callouts in a bulleted list, not narrative prose with free-form reframes. Appropriate for Key Concepts section architecture; no carve-out needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All technical terms used in M6 body are appropriate for post-M5 arc position: three-pattern (earned M5), verifier/judge/gate (earned M5), skill/eval (earned M4+), compound (earned M4+).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a module-level file, not an exercise with forcing functions trimmed to prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a module-level file, not an exercise. No recap sections present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
